bullets.text: A Ukrainian national has been charged in Germany in connection with the Nord Stream pipeline blasts, an accusation Ukraine denies, potentially straining its relationship with Germany.
The US has blocked the long-term renewal of the North American trade deal, triggering annual rolling reviews instead of a 16-year extension.
Amnesty International reports that Sudan's Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity, including ethnic cleansing, in El Fasher as part of a systemic campaign against civilians.
A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to find details about a domestic violence complaint against a friend.
Traditional owners and locals are challenging a government plan to return water to nine flood plains along the Murray River, arguing it will harm trees and wildlife.
Arts degrees in Australia are set to cost $50,000 until at least 2028, as measures to lower university fees have been put on hold.
Global dealmaking has reached a record $2.8 trillion, driven by mega takeovers as companies and investors adjust to economic shifts and the rise of AI.
Eurozone inflation fell more than expected to 2.8% in June, though price growth remains above the central bank's target for the fourth consecutive month.
The US has opted not to renew the Trump-era trade deal with Mexico and Canada, choosing instead to hold annual reviews on the terms of commerce with its biggest trading partners.
